name: chana madra
notes:
Boil the chickpeas in turmeric first. When adding chickpeas to masala with yogurt / raisins / almonds, do not add more water, the recipe is hydrophobic. The almonds also need to be soaked for a few hours before adding. Note that I think ghee makes it worse. Porcini mushroom powder is non-indian but it helps a ton by adding depth, and lemon adds brightness. Cilantro is better than kasoori if available.

ONION TREATMENT — COULD GO EITHER WAY: unlike chana masala and rajma (see basic-tomato-dal), chana madra doesn't have a clear winner between blended and coarse onions. Both work. Blended boiled onions balance the yogurt nicely (smooth-on-smooth), and that's a real argument for going that direction. But the recipe already has almonds, raisins, and peppers contributing chunky elements — this is more of a stew than a smooth gravy, and coarse onions don't read out of place. Pick whichever fits the texture you want on the day; both are defensible here.
